description: Similar titles but different dates - weekly meetings - should NOT match

content_parts.text:
You are comparing calendar events to detect duplicates.

**New Event:**
- Title: Team Retrospective
- Start: 2026-04-10T14:00:00
- End: 2026-04-10T15:00:00
- Location: Conference Room B
- Description: Sprint 12 retrospective - discuss what went well and what to improve

**Existing Events (same date):**

1. Event ID: event-001
   - Title: Team Retrospective
   - Start: 2026-04-03T14:00:00
   - End: 2026-04-03T15:00:00
   - Location: Conference Room B
   - Description: Sprint 11 retrospective meeting

2. Event ID: event-002
   - Title: Sprint Planning
   - Start: 2026-04-10T10:00:00
   - End: 2026-04-10T12:00:00
   - Location: Conference Room A
   - Description: Plan sprint 13

**Question:** Is the new event the same as any of the existing events?

Consider events the same if they refer to the same real-world event, even if:
- Wording is slightly different
- One has more details than the other
- Time changed slightly (updates)

Return the matching Event ID (or null if no match) and brief reasoning.

trace.raw_response_text: {"matched_event_id":null,"reasoning":"No match. event-001 is a similarly named retrospective in the same room but on a different date (and refers to Sprint 11 vs Sprint 12) — likely a separate recurring occurrence, not a duplicate of the 2026-04-10 instance. event-002 has a different title, time and location and is not the same event."}
